title: cissampelos pareira var. senensis
intro: Cissampelos pareira var. senensis, also known as the Indian snakeroot or Chota Chandan, is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Cissampelos pareira var. senensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and topical creams, and is often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall well-being.
